title: Guidance on labelling requirements for biocides: Template and checklist - Canada.ca
content:
## Guidance on labelling requirements for biocides: Template and checklist
- Principal display panel
- Inner and outer labels
- Labelling flexibilities
- General labelling considerations
- Template and checklist
- Labelling requirement checklist
This template is a visual representation of the information contained in this guidance document. Use this template and also follow the labelling checklist to ensure that your label text complies with the labelling requirements set out in the Biocides Regulations (regulations).
Your label text and marketed labels must also comply with the Food and Drugs Act , as well as provincial, territorial, municipal and other applicable legislation.
Principal display panel
At least 1 of the intended use or purposes (claims)
Setting(s) in which it is intended to be used
KEEP OUT OF THE REACH OF CHILDREN. / TENIR HORS DE LA PORTÉE DES ENFANTS.
READ THE LABEL BEFORE USING. / LIRE L'ÉTIQUETTE AVANT UTILISATION. (recommended)
[Aspects of safety labelling, such as hazard symbols, if required]
[Signal words and hazard statements, if required]
Sterile / stérile (if applicable)
Net quantity in the package:
Active ingredients (w/w) Active ingredient A X% Active ingredient B X%
Formulants (recommended) Formulant A, formulant B, formulant C, formulant D
Intended uses or purposes (claims)
- dilution instructions (if applicable)
- pre-clean statement (if applicable)
- method of application
- surfaces to which the biocide may be applied
- rinse and contamination statements (if applicable)
- restrictive use statements (if applicable)
- application temperature (if applicable)
- re-use applications (if applicable)
Signal words and primary hazard statements
Precautionary and prevention statements
Personal protective equipment
Physical and chemical hazard statements
First aid statements
LOT XXXXXX EXP MM-YYYY
Market authorization holder name
Questions? Email address, phone number, website address
Physical form of the biocide (if not obvious)

## Labelling requirement checklist
Labelling requirement
Outer label (if there is one)
Small package inner label Footnote 1
Bilingual (English and French)
Information is clearly and prominently displayed
Principal display panel
At least 1 of the intended uses or purposes
Settings in which the biocide is intended to be used
Identification number and DIN designation
Net quantity of the biocide in the package
If the biocide is packaged in a pressurized disposable metallic container (designated container), the hazard symbols, signal words and primary hazard statements as set out in the regulations Also refer to the Labelling Requirements for Pressurized Containers for Biocides web page Recommended for biocides packaged in any type of pressurized container
If the biocide is packaged in a pressurized disposable metallic container (designated container), the hazard symbols, signal words and primary hazard statements as set out in the regulations
Also refer to the Labelling Requirements for Pressurized Containers for Biocides web page
Recommended for biocides packaged in any type of pressurized container
Some exemptions for small quantities as set out in the regulations
Some exemptions for small quantities as set out in the regulations
Other hazard signal words (if applicable)
The statements "Keep out of reach of children." and "Tenir hors de la portée des enfants."
The statements "Read the label before using." and "Lire l'étiquette avant l'utilisation."
If the biocide is sterile, the words "sterile" and "stérile"
Additional intended use(s) or purpose(s)
Directions for use including:
Dilution instructions if applicable
Pre-clean statement (if applicable)
Method(s) of application
Surfaces to which the biocide may be applied
Rinse and contamination statements (if applicable)
Restrictive use statements (if applicable)
Application temperature (if applicable)
Re-use applications (if applicable)
Risk information, including:
Signal words, precautionary, and prevention statements
First aid statements
First aid statements
If the biocide is packaged in a pressurized container (designated container), the additional statements as set out in the regulations Also refer to the Labelling requirements for pressurized containers for biocides web page Recommended for biocides packaged in any type of pressurized container
If the biocide is packaged in a pressurized container (designated container), the additional statements as set out in the regulations
Also refer to the Labelling requirements for pressurized containers for biocides web page
Recommended for biocides packaged in any type of pressurized container
Some exemptions for small quantities as set out in the regulations
Directions for storage
Quantity of each active ingredient (% w/w)
Qualitative list of formulants contained within the biocide (in alphabetical order or descending order of quantity)
Expiry date, unless the biocide is a household biocide with a shelf life of more than 1 year and the package contains a quantity of the biocide that may reasonably be expected to be used within 1 year after it is sold
Name of the market authorization holder
Contact information for the market authorization holder allowing persons in Canada to contact them without cost (for example, email address, phone number, website address)
Physical form of the biocide (if not obvious)
N/A: not applicable w/w: weight per weight
w/w: weight per weight
